question_title: What will be the value of "ICE BofA Single-B US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread" on 2026-02-25?
summary: The ICE BofA Single-B US HY OAS was 3.19% on Feb 12, 2026. With 8 trading days to the Feb 25 resolution date, I ran Monte Carlo simulations using three approaches: bootstrap resampling of recent daily changes (3-month window), parametric normal simulation, and historical 8-day difference distribution from a 2+ year sample. All three converge on a median near 3.19% with an 8-day standard deviation of ~0.17-0.18%. The recent trend shows widening from ~2.81 in late January to 3.19, driven by tariff uncertainty, but the near-zero daily drift over the full sample suggests no strong directional bias. The distribution is roughly symmetric around the current level.

factors.description: Current value anchor: 3.19% as of Feb 12, only 8 trading days to resolution, limited time for large moves
Daily volatility ~0.055%, 8-day std ~0.185%, simulations converge on distribution centered near 3.19
Recent uptrend from 2.81 to 3.19 over ~3 weeks suggests mild upward pressure from tariff uncertainty
Market commentary suggests spreads should remain contained; no imminent catalyst for major widening
Tail risk: tariff escalation could cause sharp spread widening (Aug 2024 precedent: +75bp in 3 days)

reasoning:
Now let me run a Monte Carlo simulation using the historical data to generate calibrated percentiles.Let me also check for any recent market-moving news that might affect credit spreads.**Forecast for BAMLH0A2HYB on 2026-02-25:**

The current value is 3.19% (Feb 12). With only 8 trading days to resolution, the forecast is anchored tightly around this level.

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | 2.99% |
| 20th | 3.06% |
| 40th | 3.15% |
| 60th | 3.22% |
| 80th | 3.32% |
| 90th | 3.40% |

**Key drivers:**
- Daily volatility of ~5.5bp produces an 8-day standard deviation of ~18bp, limiting the likely range
- Three independent Monte Carlo methods (bootstrap, parametric, historical 8-day diffs) all converge on this distribution
- Recent widening from 2.81 to 3.19 was driven by tariff concerns, but the overall daily drift is near zero
- Slight upside skew reflects tail risk from potential tariff escalation (Aug 2024 saw a 75bp spike in 3 days)
